data.title: GSK wins EU backing for multiple myeloma drug, Blenrep
data.description:
GSK (NYSE:GSK) announced Friday that an expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency (EMA), endorsed its antibody-drug conjugate Blenrep as a late-line option and as part of a combination regimen for adults with multiple myeloma, a type of blood cancer.

Issuing a positive opinion, EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has recommended approval of Blenrep for adults with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma, the British drugmaker said.

The CHMP backed the use of Blenrep as part of two combination regimens: one with bortezomib plus dexamethasone (BVd) and another with pomalidomide plus dexamethasone (BPd) in those who have received at least one prior therapy.

The recommendations will next be reviewed by the European Commission (EC) from which a final decision on the drug’s marketing authorization is expected in Q3 2025, GSK said.

In 2024, the company pulled Blenrep from the European market after the regulators declined to renew the conditional marketing authorization granted for the medicine in R/R multiple myeloma over efficacy concerns. However, months later GSK renewed its bid to market the drug, and the EMA accepted its marketing authorization application in July 2024.

data.title: Ashford Hospitality to sell Houston hotel for $27M
data.description:
Ashford Hospitality Trust (NYSE:AHT) agreed to sell the Hilton Houston NASA Clear Lake for $27.0M, a transaction that will help the REIT reduce some debt and capital spending, the company said Friday.

The hotel REIT has been opportunistically selling some properties to transform the company. It has additional assets on the market at various stages of the sale process, it added.

"This sale will deleverage our Morgan Stanley 17 Pool loan, and result in significant capital expenditure savings," Ashford Hospitality (NYSE:AHT) President and CEO Stephen Zsigray said.

Ashford Hospitality (AHT) stock rose 5.8% in Friday premarket trading.

When adjusted for anticipated capital expenditures on the property, the sale price represents a  3.2% capitalization rate on net operating income, or 23.6x hotel EBITDA for the 12 months ended April 30, 2025. Excluding the anticipated capital spending, the price represents a 5.0% capitalization rate on net operating income, or 15.2x hotel EBITDA for the 12 months ended April 30.

data.title: Hyperscale Data announces acceptance of $TRUMP meme coin as payment method at indirectly-owned unit
data.description:
Operator of data centers for bitcoin mining, Hyperscale Data (NYSE:GPUS), said on Friday that the $TRUMP meme coin is set to become an accepted payment method at its indirectly-owned social-casino platform Bitnile.com.

Shares were -8.43% pre-market at $6.41.

The players will be able to use Solana-based $TRUMP to purchase a package of Bitnile.com's virtual in-game currency, Nile tokens, from about June 15.

"We love the idea that a meme coin can have real purpose and functionality in today’s digital world. With more than 750,000 wallets holding $TRUMP, we believe there is a market for those holders who want to engage in social gaming," said Milton Ault, executive chairman of Hyperscale Data.

Additionally, Hyperscale Data said BitNile.com is actively evaluating additional cryptocurrencies for future payment options.

data.title: Sibanye Stillwater tries to free 260 miners trapped underground in South Africa mine incident
data.description:
Sibanye Stillwater (NYSE:SBSW) said Friday it is working to free 260 miners trapped underground at its Kloof gold mine in South Africa after the hoist system used to access the shaft was damaged in an accident.

Sibanye (NYSE:SBSW) said a "shaft incident" had occurred Thursday at the mine, one of South Africa's deepest; the company is digging for gold at its Kloof 7 shaft at depths of nearly two miles.

The Kloof mine, which accounts for 14% of Sibanye's (SBSW) total gold output, also operates two other shafts, and the company also mines platinum-group metals in South Africa and the U.S.

All the workers are safe and gathered at an assembly point where they were provided with food while efforts are being prepared to bring them out of the mine Friday afternoon.

data.title: Amazon shareholders reject proposal separate CEO and chair roles
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) shareholders rejected a proposal that would require the company to permanently separate the roles of the CEO and the board chair, with 82% voting against the proposal at Wednesday’s shareholder meeting.

The vote was initiated by the advocacy group, The Accountability Board, arguing that the company hasn’t codified this separation into policy “giving the board the ability to freely combine the roles at any time, and establish the leadership structure that it determines best supports its operations and the company at a particular time.”  

Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) separated the roles of board chairman and CEO in 2021 when Jeff Bezos stepped down, handing over control of the company to CEO Andy Jassy. Bezos remained executive chairman of Amazon’s (NASDAQ:AMZN) board.

The board, however, argued that by maintaining the current structure, the board can “determine the right leadership for the company in light of our specific circumstances at any given time.”

“In light of our success through these various leadership structures, the board believes that shareholders are better served by the board retaining the ability to adapt to our evolving needs and implement the optimal leadership structure,” Amazon’s board said in its proxy statement, adding that “implementing the proposal would not change our leadership structure or further enhance the independent oversight the Board already exercises.”

data.title: Trump threatens 50% tariff on EU products starting in June
data.description:
Frustrated with the lack of progress in reaching a trade deal with the European Union, President Donald Trump said he'll recommend a 50% tariff on the EU starting June 1, 2025, according to his social media account.

"The European Union, which was formed for the primary purpose of taking advantage of the United States on TRADE, has been very difficult to deal with," he wrote in a post on Truth Social. "Their powerful Trade Barriers, Vat Taxes, ridiculous Corporate Penalties, Non-Monetary Trade Barriers, Monetary Manipulations, unfair and unjustified lawsuits against American Companies, and more, have led to a Trade Deficit with the U.S. of more than $250,000,000 [sic] a year, a number which is totally unacceptable. Our discussions with them are going nowhere!"

Note that the Office of the U.S. Trade Representative website said the U.S. total goods trade deficit with the EU was $235.6B in 2024, a 12.9% increased from 2023.

The president also repeated that no tariffs are charged on products "built or manufactured" in the U.S.

Earlier, Trump said that he'd place a 25% tariff rate on iPhones that aren't made in the U.S.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Wolfspeed names David Emerson as COO
data.description:
• Wolfspeed (NYSE:WOLF) said it has appointed David Emerson as its COO and executive vice president.
  • The firm added Emerson will be responsible for Wolfspeed’s operations, supply chain, and quality divisions.

data.title: PSQ Holding appoints James Rinn as CFO
data.description:
PSQ Holding (NYSE:PSQH) Friday  announced that James Rinn has been appointed Chief Financial Officer, effective June 1, 2025. 

Rinn, who has served as a board member and chair of the PublicSquare Audit Committee since July 2023, will succeed Brad Searle, who will transition to Senior Vice President of Finance for the Company’s Brands Division. 

Rinn brings over 30 years of accounting leadership at public and private companies, having held multiple CFO roles throughout his career.
